How It Works

Four Steps to A Clear Cash Offer

A cash as-is sale should feel simple from the first conversation to the closing table. Here's exactly what to expect.

1. Request Your Cash Offer

Send us the property address and a few details about the home so we can start reviewing your options. Takes two minutes online, or call us directly.

What we need:
  • Property address
  • Your contact info
  • General condition (rough idea is fine)
  • Your ideal timeline

2. We Review It As-Is

We look at the property, repair needs, local values, and your timeline so the offer fits the real situation—not a best-case scenario.

What we analyze:
  • Recent comparable sales in your area
  • Estimated repair & update costs
  • Holding costs (taxes, insurance, utilities)
  • Closing costs & title fees

3. Get a Clear Cash Number

You receive a straightforward cash offer with no seller-side commissions, repairs, cleanup, or prep needed, and no obligation to accept.

Your offer includes:
  • Offer price in writing
  • Repair estimate breakdown
  • All closing costs itemized
  • Your estimated net proceeds
  • Proposed closing date range

4. Close on Your Schedule

If the offer works, we coordinate title and paperwork so you can close in as soon as 14 days or choose a date that gives you room.

We handle:
  • Title search & clearance
  • Payoff of existing mortgages/liens
  • Closing document preparation
  • Wire transfer at closing
  • Flexible move-out coordination
Ready to start?

Step 1 Takes Two Minutes

Fill out the quick form or call us. Either way, you'll have a real conversation with a real person—no bots, no pressure.

Start My Offer Call (555) 123-4567
Common scenarios

When This Process Helps Most

Cash offers shine in situations where time, certainty, or condition matter more than squeezing every dollar.

Inherited Property

Probate delays, out-of-state ownership, or a home that needs work you can't manage from afar.

Relocation / Job Transfer

You have a firm move date and can't carry two mortgages or wait for a retail buyer.

Divorce or Separation

Clean break, split proceeds, move on. No showings with an ex in the house.

Major Repairs Needed

Foundation, roof, HVAC, mold, or outdated everything. We factor it in; you don't fix it.

Landlord Fatigue

Tenant issues, vacancy, or just done managing rental property. We buy with tenants in place.

Financial Pressure

Foreclosure risk, tax liens, medical bills, or debt. Fast close stops the clock.
